assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue with cert-manager after k8s upgrade, explaining that control plane upgrade can change webhook behavior

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides commands 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks' to identify problematic webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Offers Option 1 to delete the webhook temporarily as fastest fix, and Option 2 to set failurePolicy to Ignore

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides specific commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Labels the webhook deletion as 'temporarily' and emphasizes this under 'Immediate Fixes' section, indicating it's not permanent

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes command to check current cert-manager version and mentions upgrading to 'latest version compatible with k8s 1.32'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response mentions upgrading cert-manager to support 1.32 (not 1.30 as specified in expectation), though the concept is correct

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option 2 specifically shows setting failurePolicy to Ignore as an alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo "success"' to test pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly mention persisting changes in source of truth like Helm charts, GitOps manifests, or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While failurePolicy: Ignore is mentioned as an option, the response doesn't explicitly state the risks or recommend reverting to Fail after resolution
